title: Krishi and the Sleepy Stars

characters.name: Krishi
characters.visual_description: A 10-year-old Indian girl with long black hair in two braids tied with small red ribbons. She wears a soft yellow kurta top with tiny white flowers on it and light blue pajama pants. She is small and slim with warm brown skin and big dark eyes. She is barefoot.

characters.name: Ottu the Owl
characters.visual_description: A big, round, fluffy owl with soft brown and white feathers. He has large golden-yellow eyes and a small orange beak. He wears tiny round glasses that sit on the end of his beak. He is about as tall as Krishi's shoulder when she sits down. He always looks calm and kind.

pages.text: It was nighttime, and Krishi could not sleep. She crept outside and sat on the big old tree branch in her backyard. The moon was round and soft and glowing just for her.
pages.scene_description: A little girl sits on a wide, low tree branch in a quiet backyard at night. The full moon hangs low and bright in the sky. The yard is peaceful with soft grass below and a few fireflies glowing nearby.

pages.text: Then she heard a soft sound. Hoo... hoo... Sitting right beside her was a big fluffy owl with tiny little glasses! He smiled a slow, wise smile. 'Hello, little one,' he said. 'You are up very late.'
pages.scene_description: A large owl appears on the same tree branch next to the girl. They are sitting side by side on the branch, close together. The owl turns his big round head slowly toward the girl. Fireflies float gently around them.

pages.text: 'I cannot sleep,' said Krishi. 'My brain keeps thinking and thinking and thinking!' Ottu nodded slowly, like he had heard this a hundred times before. 'Ah yes,' he said. 'Your brain needs a little help to rest.'
pages.scene_description: The girl and the owl sit together on the branch, facing each other. The girl gestures with her hands near her head to show her busy thoughts. The owl listens with a calm, gentle expression, nodding slowly.

pages.text: Ottu pointed one soft wing up at the sky. 'Look at those stars,' he whispered. 'Even the stars get tired and yawn at night.' Krishi looked up and giggled, because she thought she saw one tiny star blink slowly, just like a sleepy eye.
pages.scene_description: Both the girl and the owl tilt their heads back and look up at the starry sky together. The sky is filled with soft glowing stars. One star in the middle of the sky appears slightly bigger and warmer than the others, as if blinking gently.

pages.text: Ottu spoke in a low, slow, sleepy voice. 'The flowers are asleep. The birds are asleep. Even the little ants are tucked in tight.' Krishi felt her eyes go heavy, just a little bit heavy. She leaned her head against Ottu's soft, fluffy feathers.
pages.scene_description: The girl leans her head gently against the owl's side on the branch. The owl sits very still and calm. Around them the garden is quiet and dark with small sleeping flowers visible near the base of the tree.

pages.text: Ottu whispered one last thing before she drifted off. 'You are safe, little one. The night is just the world giving you a hug.' Krishi smiled a soft, small smile. And just like that, she was ready to sleep.
pages.scene_description: The girl is being gently carried back toward a warmly lit house by the owl walking beside her. The back door glows with a cozy warm yellow light. The moon follows gently overhead. The scene feels safe and still and very quiet.

moral: The night is not something to be afraid of — it is just the world wrapping you up in a great big cozy hug.
